key_insight: Composition controls whether layered FeMN2 nitride films (M = W, Mo) form as highly oriented, phase-pure layered structures or as more disordered/polycrystalline microstructures, with FeWN2 showing the most robust accommodation across compositions.
engineering_relevance: These results identify sputtering plus NH3 annealing pathways for tuning phase purity, texture, resistivity, and magnetic response in layered nitride thin films for functional electronic and magnetic materials.
method: Reactive sputtering to deposit compositionally varied FeMN2 precursor films
Post-deposition NH3 annealing to form layered ternary iron nitride phases
Synchrotron GIWAXS and XAS to track phase formation, texture, and local Fe coordination
evidence_signal: GIWAXS/XAS show broad layered-phase formation in both systems, with FeWN2 maintaining high phase purity over a wide range while FeMoN2 is phase-pure mainly at Fe-poor compositions; resistivity and magnetization vary accordingly.
watch_item: The reported magnetic behavior is preliminary and room-temperature only, so the electronic/magnetic conclusions may require broader temperature- and composition-dependent validation.
paper_title: Composition-dependent Thin-film Synthesis of Layered Ternary Iron Nitrides FeMN2 (M = W, Mo)
